definitions.text: The act of speaking in a quiet voice, especially without vibration of the vocal cords; the sound thus produced.
examples: I spoke in a near whisper.
There are whispers of rebellion all around.

definitions.text: To speak softly or under one's breath, so as to be heard only by one near at hand; to utter words without sonant breath; to talk without that vibration of the vocal cords which gives sonorous, or vocal, sound.

To say (something) softly or under one's breath, so as to be heard only by one near at hand; to utter (something) without sonant breath, without that vibration of the vocal cords which gives sonorous, or vocal, sound.
To mention privately and confidentially, or in a whisper.
